method: B7 residual: Luk 12:45 (valid M08-B anchor, the unfaithful steward) uncited. Extend finding 11899 (char-15 'domineering-authority mode') — Luk 12:45 is a textbook exemplar of that mode and the Lukan twin of Mat 24:49 (already cited in 11900). Extractor re-derives the citation.

extensions.anchor: 2Pe 2:10, vc=42739: self-willed dismissal of authority and refusal to tremble).
extensions.insert_text: The unfaithful steward exemplifies the same domineering-authority mode: Luk 12:45 (M08-B-VCG-03) — presuming his master's delay, he beats the menservants and maidservants, an act of self-authorised, domineering abuse of delegated power once accountable oversight seems absent.
extensions.reason: Luk 12:45 verse_context note: the unfaithful servant beating fellow servants after assuming his master is delayed; self-elevated assumption of unchecked authority, domineering self-will, arrogant abuse of power. Directly evidences 11899's domineering-authority mode (claiming/exercising authority over others bypassing legitimate constraint); Lukan twin of Mat 24:49 already cited in 11900.
